reasoning: At 1x the card reads as a generic horizontal rectangle — the corner ornaments and top gem dissolve entirely, leaving zero charm signal. At 4x the pixel-art border and red-seal topper give it some RPG character, but the overall silhouette is stiff and rectangular, directly opposing the round/soft toy-charm target. On dark bg contrast is acceptable (golden border carries the edge). On light/white bg the beige card body nearly merges with the background — the card loses its bounding edge and looks washed out. The scattered pink dots add mild whimsy but not enough to register as 'cute'. This is functional-RPG aesthetic, not friendly-toy aesthetic: clear-but-charmless.
